root_text.an9.63:2.1: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ sikkhādubbalyānaṁ pahānāya cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an9.63:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.an9.63:2.6: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.an9.63:2.7: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ sikkhādubbalyānaṁ pahānāya ime cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an9.63:0.3: Weaknesses in Training and Mindfulness Meditation
translation_text.an9.63:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five weaknesses when you’re training.
translation_text.an9.63:1.3: Killing living creatures, stealing, sexual misconduct, lying, and consuming beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an9.63:1.4: These are the five weaknesses when you’re training.
translation_text.an9.63:2.1: To give up these five weaknesses in your training you should develop the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an9.63:2.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.an9.63:2.4: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.an9.63:2.5: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind …
translation_text.an9.63:2.6: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.an9.63:2.7: To give up those five weaknesses in your training you should develop these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.”
